articles.title: Telangana | Heatwave alert for multiple parts from April 14 to 18 - The Hindu
articles.full_text:
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued a heatwave alert for multiple parts of Telangana from April 14 and 18, warning of a gradual rise in maximum temperatures across the State over the coming days. As per IMD, the maximum temperatures are expected to increase by 2°C to 3°C over the next three days.
Heatwave forecast for three districts for Tuesday
According to IMDs latest bulletin, heatwave conditions are likely to prevail in isolated pockets in Adilabad, Nirmal and Nizamabad districts on Tuesday (April 14, 2026).
April 15
On Wednesday (April 15, 2026), heatwave conditions are likely to prevail at isolated places in Adilabad, Kumaram Bheem Asifabad, Mancherial, Nirmal, Nizamabad and Khammam districts.
April 16
On Thursday (April 16, 2026), heatwave conditions are likely to prevail at isolated places in Adilabad, Kumaram Bheem Asifabad, Mancherial, Nirmal, Nizamabad, Jagtial, Rajanna Sircilla and Khammam districts.
April 17
On Friday (April 17, 2026), heatwave conditions are likely to prevail at isolated places in Adilabad, Kumaram Bheem Asifabad, Mancherial, Nirmal, Nizamabad, Jagtial, Rajanna Sircilla, Khammam, Vikarabad, Sangareddy, Mahabubnagar and Nagarkurnool districts.
April 18
On Saturday (April 18, 2026), heatwave conditions are likely to prevail at isolated places in Adilabad, Kumaram Bheem Asifabad, Mancherial, Nirmal, Nizamabad, Jagtial, Rajanna Sircilla, Karimnagar, Peddapalli, Jayashankar Bhupalpally, Khammam, Vikarabad, Sangareddy, Mahabubnagar, Nagarkunrool, Wanaparthy, Narayanpet and Jogulamba Gadwal districts.
Hyderabad and its surrounding areas for the next 24 hours may see hazy conditions during the morning hours. The maximum and minimum temperatures are likely to be around 41℃ and 28℃ respectively, as per the bulletin.
Published - April 14, 2026 03:54 pm IST
This is a premium article, available exclusively to subscribers.

articles.full_text:
Home |Telangana |Heatwave Grips Parts Of Erstwhile Adilabad District
Heatwave grips parts of erstwhile Adilabad district
Several parts of the erstwhile Adilabad district witnessed severe heatwave conditions with temperatures crossing 41 degrees Celsius. The intense heat forced people indoors and disrupted farm work, with residents relying on cooling drinks to cope with the weather.
Adilabad: Several parts of the erstwhile Adilabad district saw scorching heatwave conditions on Tuesday.
Adilabad district recorded an average maximum temperature of 41.8 degrees Celsius. Tamsi, Bela, Bhoraj, Thalamadugu and Sathnala mandals recorded temperatures between 39 and 42 degrees Celsius. The maximum temperatures in Asifabad, Dahegaon, Bejjur, Tiryani, Kerameri, Thanur, Khanapur, Mamada, Bhainsa, Nennal, Bheemini, Mancherial and Kannepalli mandals were over 41 degrees Celsius.
Due to the unusual sweltering heatwave conditions prevailing in the middle of April, people were forced to remain indoors and stay hydrated. Roads wore a deserted look in towns. Farmers suspended farm operations. They relied on fresh juices, coconuts, ice apples and buttermilk to beat the heat.

articles.title: Severe heatwave grips Telangana as temperatures cross 43 degrees Celsius - The Siasat Daily
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Telangana
articles.full_text:
Hyderabad: Telangana is experiencing intense summer as temperatures across the state have increased sharply.
According to data from the Telangana Development Planning Society, on Monday, April 13, maximum temperatures crossed 43 degrees Celsius in two districts. In all other districts, the temperatures remained above 41 degrees Celsius.
A total of 22 districts recorded temperatures above 42 degrees Celsius, while the remaining 11 districts saw temperatures between 41 and 42 degrees Celsius. The lowest maximum temperature in the state was recorded at 41 degrees Celsius in Hyderabad.
The highest temperature was recorded in Aloor in Nizamabad district at 43.2 degrees Celsius. Gudipally in Nalgonda district followed closely with 43.1 degrees Celsius. In Hyderabad, Nampally recorded a maximum temperature of 41 degrees Celsius.
Several areas across the state reported temperatures of 42 degrees Celsius and above. These include Bhiknoor in Kamareddy at 42.9 degrees Celsius, Ichoda in Adilabad at 42.8 degrees Celsius.
Temperatures of 42.7 degrees Celsius were recorded in Asifabad in Kumaram Bheem Asifabad, Chegunta in Medak, Kalwakurthy in Nagarkurnool and Koheda in Siddipet.
Garidepalle in Suryapet recorded 42.6 degrees Celsius, while Ellanthakunta in Rajanna Sircilla recorded 42.5 degrees Celsius.
Aswapuram in Bhadradri Kothagudem saw 42.4 degrees Celsius, and Chigurumamidi in Karimnagar and Nagalgidda in Sangareddy recorded similar high temperatures.
The remaining districts in the state recorded maximum temperatures between 41 and 42 degrees Celsius.

articles.title: Rising Temperatures Hit Telangana - The Hans India
articles.full_text:
Rising Temperatures Hit Telangana
The intensity of heat in Telangana continues to rise daily. Today (Tuesday), Nizamabad recorded the highest temperature at 42.4°C, followed by Adilabad at 42.3°C and Mahabubnagar at 41°C. Hyderabad also experienced temperatures exceeding 40°C. In many areas, temperatures are currently 2 to 3 degrees higher than normal.
The heat begins to intensify from the morning and peaks in the afternoon, causing severe discomfort. Workers, the elderly, and young children are particularly affected. The Meteorological Department has warned that this hot weather is likely to persist for several more days. Authorities have advised people to avoid outdoor activities during the peak afternoon hours unless necessary.

articles.title: Telangana Heatwave 2026: Temperatures Cross 42°C in Nizamabad, Hyderabad Records Above-Normal Heat - Munsif News 24x7
articles.full_text:
Hyderabad: The Telangana heatwave 2026 has intensified across the state, with temperatures rising 2 to 3 degrees Celsius above normal levels. According to weather reports on April 14, Nizamabad recorded the highest temperature at 42.4°C, followed by Adilabad at 42.3°C and Mahabubnagar at 41°C. Even Hyderabad reported temperatures crossing 40°C.
Table of Contents
The rising temperatures have caused discomfort across Telangana, with people struggling to cope with the ongoing heatwave.
Heatwave in Telangana: Temperatures Rising Day by Day
The Telangana heatwave is becoming more severe with each passing day.
The increasing heat levels are making daily life difficult for residents across the state.
People Affected by Telangana Heatwave Conditions
The impact of the heatwave in Telangana is being felt strongly, especially among vulnerable groups.
- Workers exposed to outdoor conditions are facing difficulties
Residents are experiencing suffocation-like conditions due to extreme heat and humidity.
Meteorological Department Warning on Telangana Heatwave
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has warned that the Telangana heatwave 2026 is likely to continue for the next few days.
Advisory Issued: Avoid Afternoon Exposure
Authorities have advised people to take precautions during the Telangana heatwave:
Telangana Heatwave 2026 Continues to Trouble Residents
The Telangana heatwave 2026, with temperatures crossing 42°C in Nizamabad and above 40°C in Hyderabad, continues to affect daily life. With the IMD warning of continued heatwave conditions, residents are urged to remain cautious and follow safety guidelines in the coming days.
